situation: During sprint planning, stakeholders had different interpretations of an approval workflow within the Tax Officer Management Platform.
task: My responsibility was to align stakeholders and ensure the development team had a clear understanding of the expected workflow.
action: I conducted clarification workshops, mapped the workflow visually, documented edge cases, and converted the finalized process into user stories with acceptance criteria.
result: The team started development with aligned requirements, reducing requirement-related changes during sprint execution.

situation: Business stakeholders and technical teams had different priorities regarding implementation timelines and feature scope.
task: I needed to align both groups while keeping delivery timelines realistic.
action: I facilitated discussions focused on business priorities, clarified trade-offs, and helped break requirements into phased deliveries.
result: Stakeholders agreed on a prioritized scope and the team continued implementation without significant delays.

situation: New business requirements were introduced after sprint planning had already been completed.
task: I had to evaluate the impact and minimize disruption to ongoing development.
action: I worked with stakeholders and developers to assess dependencies, reprioritize backlog items, and communicate revised expectations.
result: The team adapted to the changes while maintaining delivery focus on the highest-priority items.

situation: During UAT, business users identified gaps between implemented functionality and their expectations.
task: I needed to understand the gaps and ensure alignment before go-live.
action: I organized walkthrough sessions, compared requirements against delivered functionality, and coordinated fixes with developers.
result: Critical issues were resolved before signoff, improving stakeholder confidence in the release.

situation: A feature required coordination between business stakeholders, developers, QA teams, and operations teams.
task: I was responsible for maintaining alignment and ensuring smooth implementation.
action: I conducted regular sync meetings, tracked dependencies, clarified requirements, and communicated progress across teams.
result: The implementation progressed smoothly with fewer communication-related blockers.

situation: A delivery milestone had a fixed timeline while several requirements were still being refined.
task: I needed to support delivery without compromising requirement quality.
action: I prioritized critical workflows, clarified high-risk requirements first, and worked closely with the team during sprint execution.
result: The team met delivery expectations while maintaining requirement clarity.
